## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On February 19, 1945, the massive, incredibly brutal and bloody Battle of Iwo Jima officially commenced during World War II. Over 30,000 United States Marines aggressively launched a massive amphibious assault on the heavily fortified Japanese volcanic island. The massive, horrific conflict lasted for 36 days, resulting in massive casualties on both sides. The highly strategic capture of the island provided the US military with a massive, critical emergency landing strip for B-29 bombers heavily executing the massive strategic bombing of the Japanese home islands.

On February 19, 1861, serfdom in the massive Russian Empire was officially and legally abolished. Tsar Alexander II issued the massive, historic Emancipation Manifesto, aggressively freeing over 23 million privately held serfs from absolute bondage to the land and their aristocratic owners. While heralded as a massive, monumental humanitarian reform, the complex, highly flawed implementation left massive populations of newly freed peasants heavily burdened with massive, unpayable redemption debts, contributing to the massive social unrest that ultimately fueled the Russian Revolution.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On February 19, 1630, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, the massive, universally revered founder of the Maratha Empire, was born in the massive hill fort of Shivneri in Maharashtra. A brilliant military strategist and massive, charismatic leader, he successfully carved out an independent Maratha enclave from the declining Adilshahi sultanate and aggressively, successfully resisted the massive, overwhelming forces of the Mughal Empire. He remains a massive, enduring cultural and political icon of fierce independence and highly efficient administration across India.

On February 19, 1915, Gopal Krishna Gokhale, a massive, highly influential political leader, social reformer, and key figure during the early Indian independence movement, passed away. As a massive, senior leader of the Indian National Congress, he heavily advocated for moderate, constitutional agitation to achieve self-rule from the British Empire. He is famously and massively remembered as the primary political mentor to Mahatma Gandhi, deeply influencing Gandhi's early understanding of massive, highly disciplined political engagement.
